architecture.summary: Imperative learning (IL) is a neuro-symbolic framework for robot autonomy comprising three reciprocally-learning modules: (1) a neural module that learns continuous representations, (2) a reasoning engine that applies symbolic logical, physical, and geometric reasoning to generate task constraints and objectives, and (3) a memory system that stores and retrieves learned symbolic rules and trajectories. Information flows bidirectionally: the neural module produces activations that the reasoning engine interprets symbolically; the reasoning engine generates symbolic outputs (rules, constraints, rewards) that guide neural learning; the memory system provides both as context. The framework formulates this coupling as bilevel optimization (BLO) enabling reciprocal updates.

biblio.title: Imperative learning: A self-supervised neuro-symbolic learning framework for robot autonomy
biblio.venue: The International Journal of Robotics Research

claims.statement: IL significantly enhances robot autonomy capabilities across path planning, rule induction, optimal control, visual odometry, and multi-robot routing
IL overcomes the label-intensive obstacles associated with data-driven approaches by leveraging self-supervised learning and symbolic reasoning
The bilevel optimization formulation of IL enables reciprocal learning over the three modules (neural, reasoning, memory)
IL takes advantage of symbolic reasoning concerning logical reasoning, physical principles, and geometric analysis to improve generalization to ever-changing environments
